Module chariot
Package chariot.api

Interface Account

All Known Subinterfaces:
AccountAuth
public interface Account
Helpers for obtaining authorization tokens
  • PKCE Authorization Code flow - oauthPKCE(scopes...)
  • Personal Access Tokens - personalAccessTokenForm(scopes...)

  • Method Details

    • oauthPKCE

      Account.UriAndToken oauthPKCE(Client.Scope... scopes)
      Use OAuth 2.0 with Proof Key Code Exchange to make it possible for a user to authorize the application to act on the behalf of the user for specified scopes.

      This authorization flow requires interaction between the user and Lichess, and that takes place on a so called "front channel" - outside of the application.

      An example usage, where an application used for creating tournaments would request a token for Client.Scope.tournament_write, could look like this, 

         Client client = Client.basic();
   var urlAndToken = client.account().oauthPKCE(Scope.tournament_write);
   ...

      The response contains an URL which can be used to ask the user to grant access. Web applications typically just redirects from the application page to the Lichess page - but for non-browser applications it is a bit trickier. It might be possible to use a WebView component, but the user would then need to enter their password into the application. A better option would be be to launch the user's system browser instead - then the user don't need to enter their password in other places than where they usually do.

      The following code uses the java.desktop.Desktop#browse support to achieve this. It has the possibiltiy to launch the user's browser and navigate to the Lichess page. If the user is already signed in, they will immediately be able to click the grant or deny options. Otherwise they need to login and will then be forwarded to the grant/deny page - all taking place without the application needing to know that the user authenticates with Lichess. 

         URI lichessUrl = urlAndToken.url();

   // Launch the user's system browser (i.e Firefox or similar),
   // and direct them to the Lichess
   Desktop.getDesktop().browse(lichessUrl);
   ...

      After the user has finished the grant process, Lichess will redirect the user's browser to a URL which was provided as a parameter in the lichessUrl query parameters. The redirect URL has been set to a localhost address which Chariot starts listening on when starting the PKCE flow. Chariot can continue to verify if the permission was indeed granted and that a access token now can be retrieved from Lichess. After this, the application can now retrieve the token from the urlAndToken and load a ClientAuth client with the token which permits creating tournaments. 

         // Blocks until user has finished grant process
   Supplier<char[]> token = urlAndToken.token().get();

   ClientAuth authClient = Client.auth(token);
   var result = authClient.tournaments().createArena( params -> ... );

   // When we are done, we can delete the token (making it no longer valid)
   authClient.account().revokeToken();
      Parameters:
      scopes - The scopes that the authorization token must be valid for
      Returns:
      The UriAndToken contains the URL to use for front channel communication, and a Supplier of the authorization token - which will supply a value after the front channel communication has succeeded.

    • oauthPKCEwithCustomRedirect

      Account.UriAndTokenExchange oauthPKCEwithCustomRedirect(URI customRedirectUri, Client.Scope... scopes)

    • personalAccessTokenForm

      URL personalAccessTokenForm(String description, Client.Scope... scopes)
      Helper method for creating Personal Access Tokens

      Note, a user must create the token manually.

      See also oauthPKCE(scopes) 

          Client client = Client.basic();
    var url = client.account().personalAccessTokenForm("Token for reading preferences", Scope.preferences_read);

    System.out.println("Please create a token at Lichess,\nusing this pre-filled form: " + url);
    System.out.println("Copy and paste the token when done");

    // Wait for the token
    String token = System.console().readLine();

    ClientAuth clientAuth = Client.auth(token);
    var email = clientAuth.account().emailAddress();
      Parameters:
      description - A description for the intended use of the token, so the user can easier recognize the token when browsing their security information.
      scopes - The pre-selected scopes for the token
      Returns:
      A URL where user can create a token, in order to then copy the token and use as input to an application.
      Example: https://lichess.org/account/oauth/token/create?scopes[]=challenge:write&scopes[]=puzzle:read&description=Prefilled+token+example

    • testTokens
